content.body: nicolo-ribaudo: since you asked earlier, I took a closer look and there is no semantic difference between `ContainsUsing` and `HasUnterminatedUsingDeclaration`. I'm fine with dropping the latter in favor of the former as it's a better name and easier to read.
content.formatted_body: nicolo-ribaudo: since you asked earlier, I took a closer look and there is no semantic difference between ContainsUsing and HasUnterminatedUsingDeclaration. I'm fine with dropping the latter in favor of the former as it's a better name and easier to read.

content.body: I just realised that if we break the spec into multiple documents (such as with Temporal), we're going to have to figure out how to get that to work with esmeta
or possibly ask the esmeta maintainers to add support
that could be as simple as making the single file generated code rather than the source of truth
